message: Hey team!:rocket: Can you believe it's been 8 fantastic years for Online Boutique?
Before we dive into anniversary celebrations, let's give a warm welcome to Maya, our new Security Analyst. She's got 5 years of cybersecurity expertise under her belt! :clap:
Hi everyone! :raised_hand_with_fingers_splayed: Thrilled to be here, especially on such a special occasion. Let's make Online Boutique even successful and secure together!
Welcome, Maya! Your experience will elevate our cybersecurity efforts.
Excited to have you, Maya! Bez has been very excited to have you on the team.
Impressive! Eager to learn from you, Maya.
:+1: Welcome Maya, pleasure meeting you.
Hi Maya! Your experience is top-notch. Cheers to you and to Online Boutique's 8-year anniversary! :partying_face: :tada:
Thanks for the warm welcome! Excited for the celebration and our anniversary party.

:star: Form a working theory
:bulb: Hint: Declare an incident.

this is selective

There is a spike in outbound connections in the edge router and the origin ip block 10.50.0.0/24. That ip range is the same as the ip block the frontend and payments are hosted on.
I think we first need to know what we are investigating me not convinced either if it is outbound or inb ound
Just ran a network security scan and our issues all seem to be relating to our network switch trying to figure out what exactly.
:star: Qualify the problem

Our investigation uncovered a critical issue: the network switch was compromised due to unpatched vulnerabilities. We have identified a BotNet in the switch and its creating outbound connections to the outside. It creates a DDOS (Distributed Denial of Service) to attack an external party
Keep investigating
P2-- an issue where we have found that intermittently we have network issues across the ethos system
*Incident Number INC10002:* P2-- an issue where we have found that intermittently we have network issues across the ethos system
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident

i am able to place orders with out any issue
We are working to identify a slow burning issue where the network is behaving weirdly we dont have any confirmed use case or users affected but it seems this is intemitteny
are there any customer reports ?
We have got complains of slowness but not anything not affectes Revenue
network switch had unpatched vulnerabilities, leaving it susceptible to known exploits. Attackers had exploited these vulnerabilities, leading to the compromise of the switch's integrity
Maya what can we do in the interim to quickly deal wtih this critical isssue
This means that The disruptions in outbound connections are affecting our internal company's users and the external global customer base.

We have idenntified that a security switch needs a critical patch that is affecting our both outbound and inbound calls teams continue to investigate

or routing the traffic to another ISP
